title: Performance, Load, Supply-Chain Gates, and Production Client Defense · Go Client Systems · Runtime Contract: Capstone Client Defense
summary: A production Go integration must defend latency, memory, concurrency, dependency provenance, rollback, and behavior under doubled load. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go capstone client defense increment in a Go 1.26 net/http production HTTP client release dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend protocol semantics, runtime behavior, security, reliability, testing, accessibility, operations, and changed-load evidence as one system. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Gate runtime patches, dependencies, tests, static analysis, vulnerability reachability, provenance, configuration, and rollback. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
